(4) The percon to bc nominatcd as Chairman shall be of outstanding merit and impeccabh integrity with no possibility of conflict of inte.est between his earlier employment and in holding the charge ofthe post of Chairman. 4. Age Limit.-The maximum age limit for nomination as Chairman shall be not exceeding fifth-six yean as on the last date for the receipt ofapplications. 5. Mode of recruitment - (i) The Chairman shall be appointed by a Search-cum-Selection Committee consisting of the following namely:- (D (iD (iiD (iY) (v) Cabinet Seffetary Secretary, Ministry of Environment, Forests and Climate Change Secretary, Department of Personnel and Training Secretary, Ministry of Science and Technology An expei member to be nominated by the Cabinet Secretary ... Chairman Member Member Member ... Member 6. Pay, rllowrnces and other conditions of servicc,{l) The Chairman shall receive pay in the pay scale of Rs. 67,000-79,000 (Higher Administrative Grade plus scale) with an annual increment at the rate ofthree percent. (2) ln addition to the pay specified in sub-rule (l), the Chairman shall be entitled to,- (a) a city comp€nsatory aliowance and house rent aliowance as are admissibie ro an A<iriitionai Secreiary io the Govemment of intiia: Provided'.ila! whsr€ a\e Chairman is aii;;ed an accommodalion bi"ne Cenral Gcvernmenl, he shaii no: be entitled to house rent allowance and shall be required to pay a licence fee as applicable to an Additional Secretary to the Government oflndia; (b) the ravelling allowance and daily allowance, in respect ofjoumeys undertaken by him in connection with his dutics as the Chairman, at the rates permissiblc in thc case ofan Additional Secr€tary to the Covemment oflndia; (c) the medical facilities as are admissible to an Additional secretary to the Govennnent of India. (3) The other conditions of service of the Chairman in the matters of allowances, leave, joining time, joining ume pay, provident fund gJatuity, age of superannuation, retirement benefits and other conditions of service shall be regulated in accordance with such rules and regulations as are for the time being applicable to officers belonging to the corresponding pay scale stationed at those places. 7. Disqualilication-No person,- (a) who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a penon having a spouse living, or O) who having a spouse living has entered into or contracted a marriage with any penon, shall be eligible for appointment to the said post : Provided that the Central Govemment may, if satisfied that such marriagc is permissiblc under pcrsonal law applicable to such person or the other parb/ to the marriage and that there are other grounds for doing so, ex€mpr any person from the operation ofthis rule. 8. Power to reLrr-Where the Central GoverDment is of opinion that it is necessary or expedi€nt so to do, it may, by order, for reasons to be recorded in writing, relax any ofthe provisions ofthese rules with respect to any class or category ofoenons.
